headline: What Cisco Stock Was Telling You About Its AI Orders
summary: You could be forgiven for glancing at Cisco (CSCO)’s financials in early 2025 and promptly falling asleep. As of its fiscal third-quarter report for FY 2025 (July fiscal ending), trailing-twelve-month revenue had grown 0.5%. It was a picture of a mature tech titan, not a rocket ship.

headline: The Real Risk Inside Arista Networks Stock
summary: If you hold Arista Networks (ANET) stock, you've been rewarded for betting on the AI boom. The shares have climbed 51% over the past year and trade near their 52-week high, a testament to the company's central role in building the networks that power artificial intelligence. But with a price-to-sales multiple of 21.9 sitting at a 10-year peak, the stock is priced for near-flawless execution. That's precisely why it's worth focusing on what could go wrong. The biggest risks for Arista aren't hypo

headline: HPE Selected for R&D Projects for U.S. DOE-Led Genesis Mission to Advance AI-Driven Innovation and Scientific Discovery
summary: WASHINGTON, July 22, 2026--HPE (NYSE: HPE) today announced it has been selected to participate in multiple key research and development (R&D) projects in the first phase of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Genesis Mission. The awarded projects focus on advancing AI innovation and scientific discovery across AI model application, performance optimization, networking, cybersecurity, and water availability.

content.title: HPE Selected for R&D Projects for U.S. DOE-Led Genesis Mission to Advance AI-Driven Innovation and Scientific Discovery

The awarded projects focus on advancing AI innovation and scientific discovery across AI model application, performance optimization, networking, cybersecurity, and water availability.

headline: MSA Safety (MSA) Appoints Octavio Marquez To Its Board Of Directors
summary: MSA Safety (NYSE:MSA) has elected Octavio Marquez to its Board of Directors. Marquez brings leadership experience from Diebold Nixdorf, Dell EMC, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, IBM, and NCR. The appointment introduces additional banking and technology sector expertise to the company’s board. MSA Safety, trading at $169.99, has seen the stock decline 2.4% over the past year, while showing a 5.0% return over three years and 11.2% over five years.
